unit: PR9 — Delete canonical root cli/skills/agents/docs/ (with test relocation)

contract.goal: Complete the canonical-root dissolution (PLAN.md § Q7, the project's defining commitment). Delete root cli/, skills/, agents/, docs/ — each is a duplicate copy of content authoritative in plugins/<plugin>/. Before deletion, relocate the 4 cross-cutting tests at root cli/*.test.ts (marketplace-manifest, parallel-work-invariant, plugin-bin-shims, skill-bodies-call-bare-commands) into plugins/commons/cli/ — these tests test relationships between plugins and have no per-plugin home. The 5th root-only test (cli/no-install-sh-refs.test.ts) is deleted entirely here rather than moved, since PR10 always planned to delete it (install.sh era is over twice over). After PR9, `find . -maxdepth 1 -type d \( -name 'cli' -o -name 'skills' -o -name 'agents' -o -name 'docs' \)` returns nothing.
acceptance_criteria: plugins/commons/cli/marketplace-manifest.test.ts exists with REPO_ROOT recalculated for the new depth (was `resolve(dirname(file), '..')` at root cli/, becomes `resolve(dirname(file), '..', '..', '..', '..')` at plugins/commons/cli/).

plugins/commons/cli/skill-bodies-call-bare-commands.test.ts exists with REPO_ROOT recalculated AND its SKILLS_ROOT iteration logic updated from `join(REPO_ROOT, 'skills')` (root) to iterate `plugins/<plugin>/skills/` (post-dissolution authoritative location).
cli/no-install-sh-refs.test.ts deleted (not moved — PR10 always planned its deletion; consolidating into PR9 since root cli/ goes away here).

Root cli/ directory deleted entirely (no files remaining, including cli/lib/draft-* relics that PR6 missed at root, cli/verbs/{griot,guild,loom}/ verb implementations PR4 stopped mirroring, and the cli/package.json + cli/<plugin>.ts entrypoints).
Root skills/ directory deleted entirely (13 skill dirs duplicating plugins/<plugin>/skills/<...>/).
Root agents/ directory deleted entirely (~20+ .md files duplicating plugins/<plugin>/agents/<...>.md).
Root docs/ directory deleted entirely (4 .md files duplicating plugins/commons/docs/<...>.md).
`npm test` passes (test count drops by the now-deleted duplicates; relocated tests still pass after their REPO_ROOT updates; skill-bodies test still passes after iterating plugins/<plugin>/skills/).
`node scripts/sync-shared.ts --check` passes (no drift; root paths weren't claimed by any active sync direction since PR4).

README.md updated if any reference to root cli/skills/agents/docs/ paths survived PR4's earlier edits (grep for `cli/`, `skills/`, `agents/`, `docs/` in README and update or remove any stale path references).
rules_applied: PLAN.md § PR9 — substrate-level canonical-root dissolution (Q7). Complement to PR10's dev-loop shim deletion (Q6b). Scope intentionally split per PLAN's 'asymmetric rollback profiles' rationale.

User decision (this session's grill) — option A: move the 4 cross-cutting tests to plugins/commons/cli/ first, then delete root cli/. Preserves coverage; commons is the substrate-foundation that cross-cutting marketplace tests belong to.

Scope consolidation — cli/no-install-sh-refs.test.ts is deleted here rather than moved-then-deleted-in-PR10. PR10's description anticipated this collapse ('removes it from wherever it ended up post-shift' implies single-removal regardless of intermediate state).

Substrate hygiene — root cli/lib/draft-* relics get deleted here as a side effect of removing root cli/. PR6 partially dissolved draft (in plugins/commons/cli/lib/) but didn't touch root; this completes that cleanup mechanically.

skill-bodies-call-bare-commands.test.ts gets a real algorithm update (not just path recalc) — its SKILLS_ROOT pointed at root skills/ which is being deleted; the test now iterates `plugins/<plugin>/skills/` to defend the same invariant across all plugin trees.
Closed-set marketplace invariant preserved (PR8's invariant); the 4 moved tests don't change the marketplace plugin count or shape, just where they live.
disqualifiers: Touching anything under plugins/<plugin>/skills/<existing-skill>/ — those skill bodies are authoritative; PR9 is about deleting the root duplicates, not editing the canonical content.
Rewriting any of the 4 relocated test files beyond their REPO_ROOT path constants (and skill-bodies' SKILLS_ROOT iteration target). The tests' invariants and assertions stay intact.
Loosening any tripwire (marketplace-manifest closed-set, parallel-work registry, plugin-bin-shims parity, skill-bodies bare-command). Move is mechanical; semantics stay identical.
Adding new tests authored from scratch (PR9 is delete + relocate, not new coverage).
Touching root bin/ shims (PR10's territory; the .gitignore'd shims stay until PR10 deletes them + their .gitignore entry).
Touching anything in projects/ beyond this unit's checkin + auto-emitted events (no manifest backfill, no archive moves).
Drift on `node scripts/sync-shared.ts --check` after the change.
Leaving any root cli/skills/agents/docs/ file behind — the maxdepth=1 find check must return nothing post-PR9.
inputs: cli/ (whole directory — deletion target after relocation)
plugins/commons/cli/ (relocation destination for the 4 cross-cutting tests)
README.md (sweep for any stale root-path references that survived PR4)

actions: Stashed orphan pr-opened event for PR8 (loom pr open writes events.jsonl AFTER the unit commit — substrate gap #3). Synced main from origin/main (was 2 commits behind after PR #25 merge). Reset cleanup branch to updated main. Popped the stash so the pr-opened event lands in PR9's first commit as bookkeeping carry.

Audited PR9 scope: discovered 5 root cli/*.test.ts files with NO counterpart in plugins/ (marketplace-manifest, parallel-work-invariant, plugin-bin-shims, skill-bodies-call-bare-commands, no-install-sh-refs). Discovered root cli/lib/draft-* relics that PR6 missed. Discovered root cli/verbs/{griot,guild,loom}/ left behind by PR4. PLAN PR9's 'rm -rf root dirs' was too simple — needs test relocation step.
Grilled user via AskUserQuestion (one question, 3 options: move tests to commons / accept coverage loss / split into multiple PRs). User chose option A (move to commons).
Drafted unit contract for PR9 with the expanded scope. Showed to user, approved as drafted.
Used git mv to relocate 4 cross-cutting tests from cli/ → plugins/commons/cli/. Used git rm on cli/no-install-sh-refs.test.ts (PR10 always planned its deletion; consolidating).

Updated REPO_ROOT path-const in 3 of 4 relocated tests (marketplace-manifest, plugin-bin-shims, skill-bodies — adding '..', '..' to the resolve() args for the new 3-level-deeper location). parallel-work-invariant.test.ts uses process.cwd() so no path-const change needed.

Rewrote listSkillFiles() in skill-bodies-call-bare-commands.test.ts to iterate plugins/<plugin>/skills/<skill>/SKILL.md instead of root skills/<skill>/SKILL.md. Renamed SKILLS_ROOT → PLUGINS_ROOT (honest rename — the constant's job changed). relativePath reporting updated to 'plugins/<plugin>/skills/<skill>/SKILL.md' for grep-friendly failure messages. Graceful skip for plugins with no skills/ subtree.
Verified 4 relocated tests pass standalone (119 tests across them) before deleting root cli/.
Used git rm -r to delete cli/, skills/, agents/, docs/ entirely. Final maxdepth check returns nothing.

Ran npm test → 637/637 across 39 test files. Test count dropped from 1125/75 to 637/39 because deleted root cli/lib/*.test.ts and cli/verbs/*/*.test.ts files were duplicates of plugins/<plugin>/cli/<...> tests. No semantic test loss.

Discovered downstream fallout: derive-panel.ts:16 hardcoded SPEC_PATH = 'docs/PANEL-COMPOSITION.md' (deleted at root). Grepped for similar hardcoded root paths in TS code → only this one hit (skill bodies use plugin-relative paths that survive the dissolution).

Updated plugins/guild/cli/verbs/guild/derive-panel.ts:23 SPEC_PATH → 'plugins/commons/docs/PANEL-COMPOSITION.md' + updated the surrounding doc comment to describe the new path + flagged the consumer-project resolution gap as a substrate-improvement followup.

Discovered failing test: plugins/guild/cli/verbs/guild/derive-panel.test.ts:284-298 ('present but unparseable') wrote fixture to <tmp>/docs/PANEL-COMPOSITION.md — updated to <tmp>/plugins/commons/docs/PANEL-COMPOSITION.md to match new SPEC_PATH.
Re-ran npm test → 637/637 green.
Verified node scripts/sync-shared.ts --check → ok (4 plugins synced).

Noted that `guild` on system PATH (installed marketplace plugin) still uses the OLD SPEC_PATH — `guild derive-panel` emits 'panel-spec-unreadable (using fallback)' until the marketplace updates. Local `node plugins/guild/cli/guild.ts derive-panel` works correctly. This is a transient developer-environment quirk; PR9 merging + plugin update will resolve.

Swept README.md: rewrote 'What's inside' table (deleted skills/, agents/, cli/, docs/ rows; refreshed plugins/<name>/ row; added plugins/commons/ row; rewrote scripts/sync-shared.ts row); deleted the 'Root cli/, root skills/, root agents/, and root docs/' inert-duplicates paragraph (this PR fulfills its prediction).
Ran panel auto-derivation via local node plugins/guild/cli/guild.ts derive-panel → evaluator-contract-fit, evaluator-react-api, evaluator-test-unit, evaluator-naming.
Spawned 4 evaluators in parallel; all 4 returned VERDICT: approved with zero findings. Panel verdict: approved.

Scope-shift detection: phase-boundary signal could fire (this is mid-Phase 3 with 2 PRs remaining; arguably the boundary signal applies to the start of Phase 3, which fired in PR8 — not here). evaluator-finding signal absent (panel clean). user-comment signal — the grill on root-only tests was scope-clarification, not plan structure change. whiteboard-contradiction absent. 0 signals; no inner-RPI.
files_touched: plugins/commons/cli/skill-bodies-call-bare-commands.test.ts (moved from cli/; REPO_ROOT recalc + SKILLS_ROOT → PLUGINS_ROOT rename + listSkillFiles() rewritten to iterate plugins/<plugin>/skills/)
cli/ (deleted entirely — lib/, verbs/, package.json, *.ts entrypoints, all .test.ts files that had plugins/<plugin>/cli/ counterparts)
plugins/guild/cli/verbs/guild/derive-panel.ts (SPEC_PATH constant + comment updated for new doc location)
plugins/guild/cli/verbs/guild/derive-panel.test.ts (fixture path updated to match new SPEC_PATH)
README.md (What's inside table rewritten; inert-duplicates paragraph removed)
projects/2026-05-21-repo-compartmentalize/events.jsonl (substrate — carries orphan pr-opened event from PR8 + new events for this unit auto-emitted by loom verbs)
corrections: Mid-execution: PLAN PR9's 'rm -rf root dirs' was too simplistic — 5 root cli/*.test.ts files had no counterpart in plugins/. Grilled user (per CLAUDE.md grill-me posture) before proceeding; user chose option A (move 4 to commons; delete the 5th since PR10 planned it). Scope expansion captured in the contract before execution. Without the grill this PR would have either silently lost test coverage or silently expanded scope by guess.

Mid-execution: derive-panel.ts:16 had a hardcoded `docs/PANEL-COMPOSITION.md` path that broke when root docs/ deleted. Not anticipated by PLAN PR9. Folded the one-line fix into PR9's diff since it's downstream consequence of the substrate move; alternative was a broken-middle state between PR9 and PR10.

changes_since_previous: PR8 (fold review-skill into commons) merged to main as PR #25. Cleanup branch reset to updated main and restarted for PR9. One uncommitted carry from PR8: a pr-opened event was written to events.jsonl by `loom pr open` AFTER the PR8 commit landed (substrate gap #3: pr-opened events are emitted out-of-band of the unit commit). This PR9 bundles that pr-opened bookkeeping event into its first commit since events.jsonl is append-only and the event represents real substrate state.
notes_for_pr: PR9 is the substrate-level canonical-root dissolution (PLAN § Q7, the project's defining commitment). Phase 3 continues into PR10 (delete root bin/ shims + .gitignore entry) and PR11 (prose sweep).

Substrate gap #3 (new this session): `loom pr open` emits the pr-opened event to events.jsonl AFTER the unit commit lands. The event is orphaned outside the originating PR. PR9's first commit bundles PR8's orphan event as bookkeeping carry. Candidate substrate fix: emit pr-opened during the same lifecycle window as the checkin (or have the verb tell the caller to stage events.jsonl before opening the PR).

Substrate gap #4 (new this session): `guild derive-panel`'s SPEC_PATH was hardcoded to root `docs/PANEL-COMPOSITION.md` — broke when this PR deleted root docs/. Fixed inline (now `plugins/commons/docs/PANEL-COMPOSITION.md`). Deeper gap: the in-repo dev workflow path (process.cwd()-relative) is different from the consumer-project workflow path (plugin-install-relative). Today derive-panel works for the in-repo path only; consumer projects running `guild derive-panel` in their own repo would hit panel-spec-unreadable unless their cwd happens to have plugins/commons/docs/PANEL-COMPOSITION.md (it won't). Candidate substrate fix: resolve SPEC_PATH from the plugin's install root, not process.cwd().

PLAN scope expansion: PR9 originally was 'rm -rf root dirs' but had to grow to include 4 test relocations to plugins/commons/cli/ (the cross-cutting tests had no per-plugin home). User explicitly authorized via grill at contract-negotiation.

Consolidation: cli/no-install-sh-refs.test.ts was deleted here instead of moved-then-deleted-in-PR10. PR10's description anticipated this collapse ('removes it from wherever it ended up post-shift' implied single-removal regardless of intermediate state). PR10's scope is now strictly root bin/ shims + .gitignore.

Developer-environment quirk: the `guild` CLI on system PATH is the installed marketplace plugin (hasn't picked up PR9's SPEC_PATH fix yet). `guild derive-panel` will emit 'panel-spec-unreadable (using fallback)' until PR9 merges and the user runs `claude plugin update`. Local `node plugins/guild/cli/guild.ts derive-panel` works correctly today. Not a code bug — a transient plugin-cache lag.

Test count drop (1125/75 → 637/39) is expected and clean: root cli/lib/*.test.ts and cli/verbs/*/*.test.ts were duplicates of plugins/<plugin>/cli/<...> tests (the duplication was an artifact of the pre-PR4 sync direction). No semantic test loss; same invariants tested in the authoritative location.
Panel: evaluator-contract-fit, evaluator-react-api, evaluator-test-unit, evaluator-naming — all approved, zero findings.
Whiteboard: skipped (Phase 3 precedent + PR-level mechanical change; PLAN.md has no `**Whiteboard**:` override block under Phase 3).
